channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This smart service saves memorable podcast moments in your inbox
item.description: Listen, I don’t know about you, but I’m generally not so big on listening. I tend to be more of a “words in front of my eyes” kind of guy when it comes to taking in information (which, as I’ve come to learn, also means I’m “an old person” by modern-day standards—hey, I’m okay with that).
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Clawdbot/Moltbot/OpenClaw is cool, but it gets pricey fast
item.description: Rarely have I more appreciated the chasm between me and Silicon Valley than I have while using OpenClaw. This new AI program, which previously went by Moltbot and before that Clawdbot, has achieved virality over the past week for its ability to control your digital life via text message.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: AI use may speed code generation, but developers’ skills suffer
item.description: There’s a lot of hype about AI coding tools and the gains developers are seeing when it comes to speed and accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Future of Critical Infrastructure: Strategies for IT/OT Security, Workforce Readiness, and Trust
item.description: During this webinar, government and industry experts will discuss how agencies are addressing IT/OT security and resilience through workforce development, pilot programs and emerging technologies.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: The need to re-think government’s digital approach
item.description: Mark Forman, the former administrator for e-government and IT at OMB, explains why it’s time for a mobile-first approach to digital transformation.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: From Meta to Samsung, global tech unleashes spending to chase AI
item.description: The world's largest tech firms show no signs of easing up on AI spending, a record wave that's propelling hardware providers like Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Netscout boosts network observability with Wi-Fi 7 monitoring, certificate lifecycle tracking
item.description: Network visibility gaps are expanding as enterprises deploy more distributed infrastructure, while at the same time, certificate lifespans are shrinking. Remote sites running on wireless connectivity often lack the same level of observability available in core data centers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Thousands of iPhone apps expose data inside Apple App Store
item.description: Researchers found Apple iOS app security flaws that expose user data and payment systems despite positive App Store reviews, according to Cybernews.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China clears Nvidia H200 sales to tech giants, reshaping AI data center plans
item.description: China has begun cautiously reopening access to Nvidia’s advanced AI processors, granting approvals to a small group of its largest technology companies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: More processor coverage on Network World: Intel news and insights | AMD news and insights With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why your next microservices should be streaming SQL-driven
item.description: “It is tempting, if the only tool you have is a hammer, to treat everything as if it were a nail.” — Abraham Maslow, 1966 Microservices provide many benefits for building business services, such as independent tools, technologies, languages, release cycles, and full control over your dependencies.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft launches its second generation AI inference chip, Maia 200
item.description: Signaling that the future of AI may not just be how many tokens an AI model generates, but how optimally it does so, Microsoft has announced Maia 200, which it described as a breakthrough inference accelerator and inference powerhouse.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The drone economy is about to take off fast: A $355B market and new rule could make drones-as-a-service the next big thing
item.description: "It allows drones to be operated beyond the pilot's visual line of sight, and it allows multiple drones to operate by one operator or pilot," McDanolds said.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Smart pill confirms when medication is swallowed
item.description: MIT engineers design smart pill that confirms medication ingestion using biodegradable antenna technology. The pill safely breaks down in stomach within days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
